text:
Great! The email was sent successfully. Now let me report back what action I took and how many incidents there were.

I found 5 high-urgency triggered incidents in PagerDuty. Since there were incidents, I did NOT post to Slack and instead sent ONE email to 'oncall@acme.io' with the subject 'Triage'. The email body listed all 5 incident IDs.

**Action taken**: Sent email to oncall@acme.io with subject "Triage" containing 5 incident IDs  
**Number of high-urgency triggered incidents**: 5
I'll check PagerDuty for high-urgency triggered incidents, then follow the specified logic. Let me start by examining the current incidents.
Now I'll filter for incidents with urgency 'high' and status 'triggered'
I found 5 high-urgency triggered incidents. According to the instructions, I should NOT post to Slack and instead send ONE email with all the incident IDs. Let me collect the IDs and send the email

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":"(def incident-ids (map :id high-triggered))\n(def email-body (str \"High urgency triggered incidents:\\n\\n\" (apply str (interpose \"\\n\" incident-ids))))\n(email__send_email {:to \"oncall@acme.io\" :subject \"Triage\" :body email-body})"}

message: unknown symbol 'interpose'. Run (tables) to list your capabilities and (describe :name) for one of them.

text: Let me use a different approach to create the body with line breaks

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":"(def incident-ids (map :id high-triggered))\n(def email-body (str \"High urgency triggered incidents:\\n\\n\" (clojure.string/join \"\\n\" incident-ids)))\n(email__send_email {:to \"oncall@acme.io\" :subject \"Triage\" :body email-body})"}
